biography: The city’s official resource for visitors. See what’s happening now in Portland! Planning a trip? DM us — we’re here to help! #LovePortland

latestPosts.caption:
You’re excited to see the sights. You’re hungry (and ready for a drink, too). But how to know what’s nearby, and which spots you simply can’t miss? Explore Portland like a local with our app-based pocket guide, curated by an expert team of local Portlanders, Near Me Now features historic sights, public art, Portland fun facts, and today’s events all over town. 

Download Near Me Now for Android or Apple.

#TravelPortland #YoursToShare #ThisIsPortland #PDX

Come along with your BURGER BESTIE @hangreeamee and @travelportland as we dive into two of the most unique burgers in PDX. First stop is @gueropdx where they’re serving an EPIC Hamburguesa. Then we head over to @jeju.pdx for their outstanding Chopped Bao Burger! 🍔 ✨ You aren’t going to want to miss this one! ☝️
•
•
•
Best Burgers in Portland, Oregon • Burgers • Chopped Burger • Burgers in PDX • #pdxnow #burgers #burgerweek #pdxeats #pdx

Let’s throw a picnic at Washington Park inside of the iconic Steven’s Pavillion - a free picnic shelter that has 10 tables & can fit up to 100 people🧺

You can hop on the free shuttle loop to get to the shelter and explore @hoytarboretum at the same time🌲

Trip Tip: Planning a picnic? Stevens Pavilion is a great spot to enjoy lunch during your visit! If you're organizing a large group gathering or special event, be sure to reserve the pavilion through Portland Parks & Recreation @portlandparks in advance.

#washingtonpark #thingstodoinportland #freeactivities #picnic #pdx

Today we’re tagging along with @sloppyjopdx for a stop at one of his favorite thrift stores, @redandfriendspdx, before heading over to @johnsmarketplace to grab a drink and cool off 🍻❤️‍🔥
#travelportland #thisisportland #portland #pdx #sloppyjopdx

🏳️‍🌈Portland Pride is here! This is your guide to the celebrations in the Rose City... Local businesses, nonprofits, friends, and allies: please drop anything we missed in the comments below so we can all add it to our weekend agenda. Have fun, everyone! We love you!🏳️‍🌈

📸: @datguy_eddy 
#LovePortland #ThisIsPortland #PDXPride

Locals and visitors: it’s time to take back the crown! To celebrate the 10th birthday of @biketownPDX, our friends at @PBOTinfo are organizing an official Guinness World Records attempt for the Largest Electric Bicycle Party and Ride. We all need YOU to make history!

Seatte holds the current record with 405 e-bikes, and we know the Rose City can absolutely shatter that.

Details:
When: Sunday, July 19th | Check-in at 8:30am, ride begins at 9:30am
Start: Salmon Springs Fountain at Tom McCall Waterfront Park
Route: A casual, slow-paced 2-mile loop over Tilikum Crossing and Hawthorne Bridge
Perk: The first 1,000 people to register get FREE entry to the Portland Pride Festival afterward, courtesy of Nike!

Bring your own e-bike, or reserve a @biketownPDX ride on a first-come, first-served basis when you sign up. (Pedal bikes are welcome to join the fun, but only e-bikes count for the record).

We’ll see you there, Portland. Let’s do this!

📸: @love_pinck / @PBOTinfo
#LovePortland #ThisIsPortland

Tuesday Food Poll: who makes the best Mediterranean food in Portland? Locals: please drop your favorites below so we can all enjoy! 
📸: @mecpdx via @snackspdx
#LovePortland #ThisIsPortland

Looking to go on a budget-friendly adventure that is perfect for all ages? An anonymous local guerrilla artist has hidden over 20 tiny, intricate metal snail sculptures across downtown Milwaukie, turning the entire neighborhood into a giant, real-world treasure hunt!
Handmade from railroad spikes, bolts, and screws, these tiny sculptures are tucked beneath benches, street poles, and crosswalks. It costs absolutely nothing to play. Just pull up the map, track down the snails with your friends or family, and input their hidden codes. If you can hunt them all down, you unlock the secret coordinates to a physical, hidden safe to claim a prize!
Drop a comment if you are ready to take on the hunt this weekend! Follow along with Kat the Whimsical Reviewer @lifted_pdx for more Pacific Northwest discovery.
#TravelPortland #MilwaukieOregon #SnailHunt #PDXFinds #DiscoverPDX

taggedUsers.full_name: Kat Darby The Whimsical Reviewer Lifted_pdx

#hawthorne #pdx #sneakers #sneakerhead #blazers basketball travelportland

IT’S TIME! One of Portland’s most scenic and audibly pleasing music festivals, the @cathedralparkjazzfestival, returns next weekend (July 17-19)! You definitely don’t want to miss this free, family-friendly extravaganza, because it’s an absolute blast. Follow @cathedralparkjazzfestival for all the details!
📷: @ninamehlhaf
#ThisIsPortland #LovePortland
PORTLAND “The city where adults feel like children and children somehow feel like adults. It’s perfect for everyone”

It’s time to explore the BEST OF patio season with @hangreeamee + @travelportland. Because what’s better than soaking up the sun in an awesome patio?! ☀️ 

Our first stop is @foxtrotpdx for low ABV cocktails and house made pie.🥧 ✨ Then we are heading over to @roadsidepdx both in the SE area of PDX. A Roadside Attraction has been a Portland patio staple for decades and it hasn’t really changed much over the years. 🍻 Check it out for yourself! 💛

#gdansk #poland #gdańsk #igersgdansk #igerspoland  #visitpoland #visitgdansk #oldtown #balticsea #polandtravel

Here comes a Monday motivation with today's #repost from @ruthlessbeast101. ⁠

 Don’t try this at home: this crazy dude is fearlessly running down backwards over the benches at the Red Rocks Amphitheatre! The famous outdoor stage is in Morrison in the US state of Colorado. U2’s Live concert film – U2 Live at Red Rocks: Under a Blood Red Sky was recorded here in 1983.⁠ ⁠ 

#redrocks #redrocksamphitheater #colorado #morrison #denver #rockymountains #us #usa #beautifulcolorado #coloradomountains #coloradosummer #concert #livemusic #music

You can't really miss this Berlin landmark. But sometimes you have to search a bit. Good morning from the German capital.⁠
⁠
#berlin #fernsehturmberlin #televisiontower #tvtowerberlin #tvtower #berlincity ⁠#visitberlin #igersberlin @berliner_fernsehturm #bestviewofberlin😍  #berlininstagram #iloveberlin #berlin365 #bestofberlin

🏰After five years of restoration, Neuschwanstein castle is shining in a new splendor. The Throne Hall which is the magnificent centerpiece of this fairytale castle, and other rooms have been restored. Dust, dirt and, above all, moisture have taken their toll on the castle and its precious inventory since the foundation stone was laid over 150 years ago.⁠
⁠
⁠
#neuschwanstein #neuschwansteincastle #schlossneuschwanstein #schloßneuschwanstein #germancastles #europeancastles #castlesofeurope #castleonthehill #fairytalecastle #romanticarchitecture #gothicrevival🏰 #visitbavaria #germany #castle #waltdisney #magicalplace #disney

Today's #repost comes from @anananwar⁠.⁠
⁠
Crucial for influencers: No matter how you come down the stairs — it has to look cool! YOU have to look cool! But is this real? 
⁠
#santorini #greece #parkour #influencer #travel #travelling #travels #instagramvsreality #oops #stunt #cool #instatravel #instagramvsreallife⁠

Warm summer greetings from Schönhauser Allee in Berlin’s Prenzlauer Berg. Yesterday we enjoyed a toasty 37° Celsius, but today we expect only 32° Celsius. How high are temperatures where you are right now?⁠
⁠
#berlin #schönhauserallee #prenzlauerberg #germany  #berlintheplacetobe #berlinstagram #berlincity #travel #berlinlove #berlinstyle #berlingram #igersberlin #instatravel #berlinsummer #summer #citylife #bestofberlin

Today's #repost comes from @explorarelperu.⁠
⁠
Gloriously colorful: Doña Santusa and her beautifully adorned Alpacas go perfectly with the Montaña de Colores in the south of Peru. Santusa earns a little extra during her old age by posing for photos at the viewing point. You can take a day trip from the village of Quechuyno, located to the south-east of Cusco, to get to Vinicunca, at 5,200 meters altitude in the Andes.⁠
⁠
#vinicunca #peru #rainbowmountain #cuscoperu #southamerica #perutravel #visitperu #visit #quechuas #quechua #andes #andesmountains #exploring #trip #hiking #travel #traveller #alpaca #andeanpeople ⁠

Today's repost comes from @rudyalceda.⁠
⁠
⁠If you want to get around Taxco de Alarcón in Mexico safely, you’ll need to be a pretty good driver. But it’s totally worth it! The silver city in the federal state of Guerrero, between Acapulco and Mexico City - delights visitors with its many windy lanes. Have you ever been there?⁠
⁠
#mexico #taxco #guerrero #taxcoguerrero #guerreromexico #pueblomágico #pueblomagico #pueblosmagicos #pueblosmagicosdemexico #mexicotravel #travelmexico #travel  #mexicotrip @visit.taxco

Good morning! We start the day with a panoramic view from the Waldbühne Berlin! The Rolling Stones will perform in this open-air arena in the Olympiapark Berlin tonight. Have you ever seen one of this legendary band’s concerts? Care to tell us where?
⁠
#berlin #waldbühne #charlottenburg #olympiapark #rollingstones #stones @therollingstones @waldbuehneberlin #openair #concert #tour #music #berlintheplacetobe

Today's #repost comes from @bhupendra_1204.⁠
⁠
A social media hotspot in India – Yamuna Ghat with its many seagulls! This bank of the holy river of Yamuna used to serve primarily religious purposes. These days most visitors come for the photo opps. It’s easy to get there on the metro, exit at Kashmere Gate station.⁠
⁠
#delhi #yamunaghat #yamuna #yamunariver #india #delhigram #incridibleindia #delhiphotography #travelindia #delhicity #exploredelhi⁠

latestPosts.caption:
Welcome to the Middle Ages in Germany! Join us on a trip to Wartburg Castle, Eltz Castle and the historic town of Bamberg. 
#germany #wartburg #eltzcastle #bamberg

On July 15, 1946, the first CARE packages from the United States arrived in Germany, bringing food, hope and relief to people in the post-war country. Eighty years later, we visit Berlin’s Allied Museum to explore the story behind the iconic packages.
#care #germany #WWII #usa #dwtravel #dwhistory

In Germany, rules aren’t suggestions – they’re a way of life. From red lights on empty streets to silent Sundays: welcome to the country where order is the social contract.
#germany #rules #rulebook #order #dwtravel

Fly above Pompeii, where echoes of ancient Romans still linger.
#pompeii #vesuvius #italy #dwtravel #BirdsEyeEurope

From crystal-clear alpine lakes to cascading waterfalls and dramatic fjords - Europe's waters are as diverse as they are breathtaking. Discover Königssee's pristine depths, Croatia's ever-changing Plitvice Lakes and Norway’s waterfall Vøringsfossen.
#königssee #germany #plitvicelakes #croatia #voringsfossen #norway #dwtravel

More than 400 French firefighters worked through the night to contain a wildfire in the historic Fontainebleau forest south of Paris. Authorities sent two water-bomber planes on Monday to tackle the blaze as a heatwave gripped western Europe. 
 
#dwnews #France

Would you trust a driverless car to get you to kickoff? Robotaxis are now part of the World Cup 2026 experience in more than half of the U.S. host cities. But how safe are they?
#driverless #robotaxi #dwsports #dwtravel

Is Brussels really as beautiful as Instagram makes it look? We checked out three famous spots to see whether reality matches the feed.
#brussels #instavsreality #belgium #dwtravel
Shirtless men in the club: dancefloor vibe ruiner, or expression of freedom? There's a debate heating up in Berlin. #dwberlinfresh #dwtrends

Which famous Berlin spots are worth visiting, and which can you skip? Berlin local Hannah Hummel shares her tips — from Alexanderplatz and Checkpoint Charlie to the East Side Gallery and the TV Tower. Which place in Berlin is YOUR favorite?
#berlin #alexanderplatz #brandenburggate #reichstag #checkpointcharlie #dwtravel

Explore Tallinn through towers, hilltop views, and centuries of history carved into stone streets.
#tallinn #estonia #BirdsEyeEurope #dwtravel

You take it everywhere when you travel the world — but how much do you actually know about your passport? Here are some interesting and surprising facts!
#passport #travel #traveling #dwtravel

latestIgtvVideos.caption: T+L Editor in Chief @jacquigiff was live from Charleston, South Carolina to celebrate the launch of the 2022 World’s Best Awards! In honor of @explorecharleston winning Best City in the U.S. for the 10th year in a row, we chatted with local business owners about what makes it so special. A big congrats to all of the winners. 🎉 #sponsored

There is absolutely nothing like heading out for a vacation to Walt Disney World. Whether it's your millionth visit or your first time, the magic remains the same. Though every trip to the Mouse House is unique, it is a good idea to be prepared. Watch for everything you need to know before you go, and head to the link in bio for more. ✨ #sponsored by @waltdisneyworld

Our annual Global Vision Awards are here! The companies, individuals, destinations, and organizations recognized are taking strides to develop more sustainable and responsible travel products, practices, and experiences around the world. From a farm animal sanctuary in Upstate New York to a luxury hotel group leading coral propagation in the Maldives, they're changing travel for the better. Read about the honorees at the link in bio.

Hilton Head's beautiful beaches and slow pace of life have drawn families and travelers for decades. Want to give #lowcountrylife a try? Tag your travel buddy in the comments. 😎👙 #sponsored by @visithiltonhead

This week, Genealogist Nicka Sewell-Smith (@neeksmith) answers @kelleesetgo's questions about tracing family histories through DNA testing and heritage travel. They discuss how families can not only trace their roots, but also reconnect with long-lost relatives, all through DNA technology.
.
Click the link in bio to listen on Apple Podcasts, or listen wherever you get your podcasts.
.
#sponsored

Do you visit small businesses when you travel? Doing so is a great way to tap into the local culture, interact with natives, and of course, find memorable souvenirs. But those aren't the only reasons to shop small. Read more at the link in bio, including local businesses you can order from online, profiles on resilient owners and their shops, and more. 🛍️

This week, @kelleesetgo sits down with Corinne Rice-Grey Cloud (@misscorinne86), an educator, activist, and journalist, to talk about her work in diversity and inclusion for Indigenous voices as well as what to keep in mind when traveling to Indigenous spaces.
.
Click the link in bio to listen on Apple Podcasts, or listen wherever you get your podcasts.
.
#sponsored

latestIgtvVideos.title: Go On an Underwater Adventure in Bermuda With This Local
latestIgtvVideos.caption: “Being a local in Bermuda, to me, is definitely a blessing,” says Weldon Wade (@weldonwade), an avid scuba and free diver. “I wake up every day thankful to be living in such an amazing, beautiful place.” On this episode of Locals, Wade shows us Bermuda from a native’s perspective. He takes us scuba diving at shipwrecks off the island’s south shore, to his absolute favorite restaurant, and more. Come along! #sponsored

latestIgtvVideos.title: Experience Wildlife at Home With This Video of the Animals of Australia
latestIgtvVideos.caption: Being confined to your home can make it seem impossible to get in touch with nature, especially if you live in a city. That’s why we’ve decided to bring nature to you! Take a few minutes to relax with us and escape the anxiety of these uncertain times. All you have to do is turn up the volume and enjoy the animals of Australia, from koalas cuddling their young to kangaroos hopping across open fields.

latestIgtvVideos.title: Experience Wildlife at Home With This Video of the Animals of Costa Rica
latestIgtvVideos.caption: Being confined to your home can make it seem impossible to get in touch with nature, especially if you live in a city. That’s why we’ve decided to bring nature to you! Take a few minutes to relax with us and escape the anxiety of these uncertain times. All you have to do is turn up the volume and enjoy the animals of Costa Rica, from birds chirping overhead to frogs croaking in the distance.

💡London has been ranked the world’s best city for the 11th consecutive year by Resonance’s World’s Best Cities report, thanks to its mix of history, culture, green spaces, restaurants, and global connectivity. From iconic landmarks to world-class museums and neighborhoods, London continues to top travelers’ bucket lists year after year.

🇬🇧 Have you visited London yet, or is it still on your travel wishlist?

💡England is reportedly moving toward restricting deep-fried foods in school menus as part of a wider push to improve children’s nutrition and reduce long-term health risks such as obesity and heart disease. The idea behind this kind of policy is not just to remove certain foods, but to reshape early eating habits during school years when dietary patterns are formed. Healthier school menus typically aim to increase fresh vegetables, whole grains, lean proteins, and reduce excessive oil, salt, and processed ingredients. 🍟🇬🇧

Do you think schools should take full responsibility for shaping children’s eating habits, or should that responsibility stay mainly with families?

💡The ranking, compiled by Australian comparison site Compare the Market AU, analyzed 50 major cities using nine family-focused factors, including safety, education spending, parental leave, green space, cost of living, and the number of child-friendly activities. London finished second behind Brisbane, earning particularly strong scores for its 758 family-friendly attractions, extensive green spaces, and generous parental leave policies. The study gave London an overall score of 5.992 out of 10, making it the highest-ranked European city in the index. 🙌🇬🇧

Do you think the ranking is considered fair? 👇

💡The Seven Sisters Cliffs are one of England’s most breathtaking coastal landscapes and can be reached in around two hours from London by train and bus. Known for their dramatic white chalk cliffs, scenic walking trails, and incredible sea views, they’re a perfect escape for anyone looking to trade the city for nature. 🌊🤍

Would you add the Seven Sisters to your London bucket list? 👀👇

🎤🧑‍🚀 LUXURY PRIVATE ROOM KARAOKE EXPERIENCE IN LONDON! 🪩🎶 @u9_london

Looking for the ultimate karaoke night in London? 👀 Hidden just moments from Leicester Square, U9 Karaoke takes singing to the next level with luxurious private rooms, incredible sound systems and a huge library of over 450,000 songs! 🤯💿 

Each room has its own unique futuristic space themed design, creating an immersive atmosphere that’s perfect for birthdays, date nights, celebrations or a night out with friends! 🥳 Enjoy premium cocktails, bottle service and delicious food delivered straight to your room so you can keep the party going without missing a song! 🍹🍾

The East Asian inspired menu is well worth trying! 🤤 Highlights include the fish tacos, tiger prawns, crispy duck rolls, spicy noodles and fried rice! 😍 Every dish was beautifully presented, packed with flavour and made to a really high standard! 👏🏼 Whatever you do, don’t leave without ordering the fruit platter, it glows in the dark making it the perfect way to end the night! 🍉😯

U9 is taking karaoke to the next level, it’s easily the best karaoke spot around! 🤩 The whole experience feels so luxurious and whether you’re a confident performer or just there for the laughs, you’re guaranteed an unforgettable evening! 😎 100% recommended, enjoy! 👽

⚠️ NOTE: Valid ID is required for entry 🪪

📍47 Whitcomb Street, London WC2H 7DH

•••

latestPosts.caption:
☀️ Dubai is heating up, and so is our event calendar! ☀️

Don’t let the summer sun keep you indoors, there is so much happening across the city this July! Whether you’re looking to shop ‘til you drop, catch a live show, or stay active, we’ve got you covered.

Here are the top events you absolutely cannot miss this month:
🛍️ Dubai Summer Surprises (July 2 – Aug 30): The city’s biggest festival is back! Expect massive citywide discounts, mega raffles, concert series, and incredible hotel and dining deals across Dubai’s top malls.

🎶 Beat The Heat DXB (July 11 – Aug 8): Catch some of the region’s biggest stars, including Cairokee and Dystinct, at this epic indoor concert series at the Dubai World Trade Centre. 🎤

🏃 Dubai Mallathon (Daily until Sept 15): Who said you can’t hit your fitness goals in the heat? Malls across the city are transforming into indoor walking and running tracks every morning!

⚽ FIFA World Cup 2026 Fan Zones (Until July 19): Feel the stadium energy! Join the crowd at the Dubai World Trade Centre or Dubai Festival City Mall for live matches on giant screens, food, and non-stop entertainment. 🥅

🕯️ Candlelight Concerts (July 11): Experience magic under the glow of thousands of candles. Enjoy breathtaking live classical tributes to legends like Queen, Hans Zimmer, and Fairuz. ✨

Which of these are you heading to first? Let us know in the comments below! 👇

#DubaiLife #DubaiSummer #DSS2026

Dubai has been named the world’s most beautiful city at night for 2026! 🌃✨ 

According to a 2026 study by JB.com, Dubai claimed the No. 1 spot for its after-dark appeal, ranking ahead of cities like Tokyo and Rome.

The study evaluated destinations based on several factors, including nighttime safety, scenic viewpoints, social media popularity, light pollution, sky brightness, and the variety of nighttime experiences available to visitors.

Dubai’s iconic skyline played a major role in securing the top ranking. From the illuminated Burj Khalifa and the spectacular Dubai Fountain to the glittering skyline of Dubai Marina, Palm Jumeirah, and the city’s vibrant nightlife, Dubai continues to redefine what a city looks like after sunset.

Beyond its famous landmarks, the city offers everything from rooftop lounges and waterfront dining to desert night experiences and late-night shopping, making it one of the world’s most dynamic destinations once the sun goes down.

Whether you’re admiring the skyline from an observation deck, cruising along Dubai Marina, or watching the city lights reflect off the Arabian Gulf, it’s easy to see why Dubai stood out in this global ranking.

💬 Do you think Dubai deserves to be crowned the world’s most beautiful city at night, or is there another city that wins your vote? Let us know in the comments! 🌙✨

taggedUsers.full_name: Tripscout | DMO Social Media Marketing Solutions
Tripscout | DMO Social Media Marketing Solutions

latestPosts.caption:
Dubai has officially opened its first dedicated women-only beach, offering a new private space designed exclusively for women to relax, swim, and enjoy the coastline! 🏖️✨

Located at Al Mamzar Beach, the new women-only section is part of Dubai’s major beachfront redevelopment project. The area features privacy fencing, female lifeguards and staff, strict no-photography rules, and even dedicated facilities for night swimming.

The beach is designed to provide a comfortable and secure environment while remaining part of the wider Al Mamzar waterfront destination, which also includes walking paths, sports facilities, family areas, and water activities.

For women looking for a more private beach experience, this new addition offers an alternative to traditional public beaches while supporting Dubai’s goal of creating more inclusive and accessible recreational spaces.

📍 Al Mamzar Beach, Dubai 🌙 Includes night swimming facilities 📵 No photography allowed in the women-only zone 👩 Female staff and lifeguards on-site

Would you visit Dubai’s new women-only beach? 👇

Dubai Mall has updated its summer dress guidelines, encouraging visitors to dress respectfully while enjoying one of the world’s most visited shopping destinations! 🇦🇪👕

As Dubai welcomes millions of tourists during the busy summer season, the mall is reminding guests to wear modest clothing in public spaces as a way of respecting the UAE’s local customs and cultural traditions.

The guidance encourages clothing that is appropriate for a family-friendly environment, helping create a comfortable and welcoming experience for both visitors and residents.

The reminder has been widely welcomed by many members of the community, who say it reflects the UAE’s cultural identity and values while promoting mutual respect between people from different backgrounds. Supporters believe the guidance helps preserve local traditions and encourages visitors to be mindful of local etiquette without affecting Dubai’s reputation as an open and welcoming destination.

While Dubai is known for its diverse and international atmosphere, visitors are generally encouraged to dress more modestly in shopping malls, traditional neighborhoods, places of worship, and government buildings. Beachwear is best reserved for beaches and hotel pools.

✨ Understanding local customs is one of the easiest ways to enjoy a smooth and respectful visit, and a small gesture that helps make everyone’s experience more enjoyable.

💬 Do you think it’s important for travelers to follow local dress customs when visiting another country? Share your thoughts below. 👇

#Dubai #DubaiMall #UAE
Still paying full price for hotels in Dubai? Find incredible stays right now, and @hotel scoured the internet to get you the best deals. Link in bio 🏨✨

Dubai is taking another step toward the future of transportation. Residents can now book driverless taxi rides starting from just Dh5 as the city expands its smart mobility network! 🚖🤖 

The new autonomous taxi service is being rolled out by Dubai’s Roads and Transport Authority (RTA) in partnership with Apollo Go, the autonomous ride-hailing platform developed by Chinese tech company Baidu. The service is currently available in Umm Suqeim and Jumeirah, with plans to support Dubai’s long-term vision of making autonomous transport a key part of everyday travel.

The self-driving vehicles rely on a combination of artificial intelligence, high-definition digital maps, cameras, radar, LiDAR sensors, and deep learning technology to detect their surroundings, navigate traffic, and make real-time driving decisions. The goal is to provide a safe, efficient, and convenient travel experience while reducing congestion and improving road safety.

Booking a ride is simple: download the Apollo Go app, choose your pickup and drop-off points within the service area, and use the one-time password (OTP) provided in the app to unlock the vehicle before your journey begins.

As Dubai continues investing in next-generation transport solutions, driverless taxis are expected to play an increasingly important role in the city’s vision of becoming one of the world’s smartest and most innovative cities.

📍Would you hop into a driverless taxi, or would you still prefer a human behind the wheel?

6 Dining Spots You Should Try at Dubai Mall via @aktravells! 🇦🇪✨

💡Planning a visit to Dubai Mall? Here are 6 must-try restaurants offering everything from global flavors to stunning views:

📍 Blume – A floral-inspired dining spot known for its vibrant ambiance and diverse international menu.
📍 Amaya – Stylish setting with a fusion menu and terrace views, perfect for a relaxed yet upscale experience.
📍 Tribes Carnivore – A meat lover’s destination serving African-inspired grills and premium cuts.
📍 The Grill Shack – Casual American-style dining featuring steaks, burgers, and hearty comfort food.
📍 KATA – Japanese cuisine with sushi, robata grills, and contemporary flavors in a sleek setting.
📍 Mara Lounge – A chic lounge experience offering Mediterranean-inspired dishes and a vibrant atmosphere.

✨ Which one are you trying first? Let us know below 👇

A huge shoutout to the incredible creator @aktravells! ❤️

📍Dubai Mall, Dubai, UAE
Still paying full price for hotels in Dubai? The best deals are waiting, and @hotel found them for you. Link in bio 🏨

flydubai is ramping up its Bangkok service starting July 18, 2026, with a brand new second daily flight from Dubai. This brings the airline's total weekly Bangkok schedule to 21 flights, reflecting strong traveler demand for Thailand connections. The expanded service operates under a codeshare partnership with Emirates, giving passengers seamless onward connections and an upgraded onboard experience. With more flight options, travelers now have greater flexibility for timing their trips to Bangkok and beyond. This expansion underscores Dubai's role as a major international aviation hub bouncing back with enhanced regional and global connectivity.

biography: Most travelers struggle to choose their own path. We equip them with a roadmap to design the journey of their dreams.

latestPosts.caption:
From New York City to Napa Valley and Indianapolis to Houston, events and festival organizers are curating safe spaces for us to gather, party, play, and enjoy the best of our music, food, and culture 🥳💃🏾

During these events, Black people come together, prioritize enjoyment, and make lifelong memories. Whether you attend alone or with loved ones, you’ll experience the unique, undeniable, and unrelenting spirit of Black joy. Plus, you’ll leave with an even broader connection to community and likely some new friends.

🔗 For the list, be sure to click the link in bio

🌊 San Diego has been lowkey one of the best cities for Black travelers. Brittany from the Travel Noire Creator Collective just made it official.

Perfect weather. Beautiful beaches. Black-owned spots worth every dollar. And a city that moves at exactly the right pace. 

Watch the full video for the real local guide and download the Travel Noire App for more City Guides 📲 

#TravelNoire #SanDiego #California #BlackTravel #TravelWhileBlack #BlackTravelMovement

🍽️ Houston's Black-owned restaurant scene is one of the best in the country. And we just built the list to prove it.

From a bakery in Third Ward that has been feeding Houston since 2000, to West African jollof jambalaya in Montrose, to the iconic Breakfast Klub line that has been wrapping around the block since 2001. Houston does not miss.

Here is a taste of what is on the list:

🥞 Dandelion Cafe: Breakfast all day, cornbread-buttermilk waffles, and a line that tells you everything you need to know
🌍 ChòpnBlọk: West African and Creole collide in the best way at this Montrose gem
🎂 Ms. Myrtle's Bakery: Third Ward institution since 2000. The Heavenly Butter Bundt Cake is exactly what it sounds like
🍗 Lucille's: Southern cooking shaped by international techniques, named in honor of a chef, educator, and inventor
🥔 Triple J's Smokehouse: Smoked over post oak since 1994. The Big J Potato is the move.
🌊 Gatlin's Fins and Feathers: Chargrilled oysters, Gulf seafood, and Southern comfort from the Gatlin family

Read the full guide at travelnoire.com!

And if you have a favorite Black-owned restaurant in Houston we missed, we want to know about it. Submit it for a Travel Noire Award at the link in bio. 🔗 Houston, show us what you have. 🔗 

#TravelNoire #Houston #BlackOwned #BlackOwnedRestaurants #HoustonFood #BlackTravel

✈️ Delta just made business class more accessible. But before you book, read the fine print.

Delta now offers Basic fares in its premium cabins, including Delta First, Delta Premium Select, and Delta One. That means lie-flat seats, three-course meals, hot towel service, complimentary drinks, and a Grown Alchemist skincare amenity kit at a lower entry price than before.

Sounds like a win. Here is what you are giving up:

🪑 No seat selection until check-in (you can pay for one depending on availability)
🧳 Reduced checked luggage allowance
✈️ No same-day flight changes or standby options
🔄 No complimentary or paid upgrades
🏆 Fewer SkyMiles earned
🛋️ Starting January 19, 2027: no automatic Delta Sky Club or Delta One Lounge access based on ticket type alone

The in-flight experience is identical to what full-fare premium passengers get. The restrictions all happen before and after takeoff.

If you fly Delta One for the seat and the meal and you are flexible on timing, this could be a serious upgrade hack. If you rely on lounge access, SkyMiles, or flight flexibility, the full fare is worth it.

Read the full breakdown before you book. 🔗 Read more at travelnoire.com

#TravelNoire #Delta #DeltaAirlines #BlackTravel #TravelWhileBlack

✈️ The data is in. Black travelers are choosing PEACE this summer. And leaving just enough room for a little something extra. 👀

BLK surveyed over 1,000 Black travelers for their 2026 Summer Travel Report and the results are giving exactly the energy we expected.

Here is what the community is saying:
🧘🏾 52% say their main reason for traveling this summer is to relax and get peace of mind
🌍 Top international destinations: Jamaica, Nigeria, Puerto Rico, the Bahamas, and Paris
🏙️ Top domestic destinations: Las Vegas, Miami, New York, Chicago, and Atlanta
💼 35.6% are traveling solo this summer
💘 78% say they are open to a summer fling. And 43% said they would definitely have a rendezvous because "that's part of the fun"

Rest is the reason. Romance is the bonus. And our community is moving accordingly. 😌

Drop your summer destination in the comments and tell us: are you traveling for peace, for a fling, or both? 👇 

🔗 Full report at blavity.com

#TravelNoire #BlackTravel #SummerTravel2026 #BlackTravelers

🇯🇵 Japan is one of the most incredible countries on earth. And there are a few things every Black traveler should know before landing.

Blavity Creator Collective traveler, YaYa shares some important tips and we added even more so you're prepared!

Japan is generally very safe, incredibly efficient, and most travelers report feeling welcome and genuinely curious energy from locals rather than hostility. But there are real things to prepare for.

Here is what you need to know:

🏨 Some traditional ryokan and onsen (hot spring baths) have historically turned away non-Japanese guests. Call ahead or book through platforms that confirm acceptance policies for international visitors.

💴 Japan is still largely a cash society. Carry yen. Many smaller restaurants, temples, and shops do not accept cards.

🚇 IC cards like Suica or Pasmo make navigating trains seamless. Load one at the airport the moment you land.

🗣️ English signage is widely available in major cities but limited in rural areas. Download Google Translate with Japanese offline before you go.

✨ Your natural hair, braids, locs, and protective styles will likely draw genuine curiosity and compliments. Most interactions come from a place of fascination, not disrespect, but you may be photographed or approached more than you expect.

📱 Pocket WiFi or a Japanese SIM card is non-negotiable. You will need navigation constantly.

Japan rewards prepared travelers more than almost anywhere else. Comment your own tips/questions below ⬇️ 

#TravelNoire #Japan #BlackTravel #JapanTravel  #TravelTips

📱 We know you want to post. But before you do, read this first.

Even the most innocent travel posts can put you at risk, and this is especially true for solo travelers and Black women traveling alone. Here are 5 things you should never share online before or during your trip:

🚫 Your itinerary: Real-time location sharing makes it easy for anyone to track your movements

💍 Your valuables: Posting cash, jewelry, or expensive gear makes you a target

🏙️ Your hotel room view: With modern technology, a window shot can reveal your exact location

🏨 Unique hotel features: Distinctive lobbies, pools, and hallways are more recognizable than you think

🎫 Your hotel wristband: The name, color, or shape can tell strangers exactly where you are staying

The golden rule? Wait until after you have left a location before posting that you were there. Even if your account is private, the hidden security risks of sharing sensitive information online are very real.

Save this. Share it with your travel crew. And comment some of your own safety tips below ⬇️ 

#TravelNoire #TravelSafety #BlackTravel #TravelWhileBlack #

🚢 The world’s largest cruise ship is about to dock in the United States. And we have everything you need to know before it does.

Royal Caribbean’s Legend of the Seas is currently sailing the Mediterranean and it is breaking the internet. Creator videos, ship tours, and aerial footage of this floating city have been going viral for weeks. Now U.S. travelers want to know: when can we board?

Here is your full breakdown:
Legend of the Seas measures 248,660 gross tons and holds up to 7,600 passengers. It has 8 neighborhoods, 7 pools, 10 whirlpools, a waterpark, an ice arena, 28 dining venues, and Crown’s Edge sitting 154 feet above the ocean.
🇪🇸 Right now: Seven-night Mediterranean sailings from Rome or Barcelona through October 18, starting at $1,480 per person
🌊 Coming soon: Legend of the Seas arrives in Fort Lauderdale in November 2026 for Western and Southern Caribbean sailings, including stops in Curacao, Aruba, and Perfect Day at CocoCay, starting at $1,582 per person
Your move: fly to Europe now or wait for Florida in November. Either way, you need to read the full breakdown before you book.

🔗  Get all the details at travelnoire.com

 #TravelNoire #LegendOfTheSeas #RoyalCaribbean  #BlackTravel

The "maybe I should move to Africa" conversation just got a whole lot more specific. And we built the guide.

For Black Americans, the right destination depends on your income, your language, your ancestry, and the legal door you can actually walk through. Here is a quick breakdown:

🇬🇭 Ghana: The clearest all-around diaspora route. The Right of Abode program gives people of African descent the legal right to live, work, and enter visa-free permanently.

🇧🇯 Benin: A formal citizenship pathway for Afro-descendants who can prove genealogical ties to the transatlantic slave trade. French-speaking. $100 application fee.

🇲🇺 Mauritius: Best for remote workers and anyone earning income from outside the country. Premium Visa allows stays up to one year with renewal.

🇰🇪 Kenya: Digital nomads and remote employees working for foreign companies can apply for the Class N Digital Nomad Permit in Nairobi.

🇿🇦 South Africa: Remote Work Visitor Visa covers stays up to three years for higher earners with a foreign employment contract.

🇳🇬 Nigeria: Makes the most sense when roots, family, professional purpose, or industry ties already point there.

Save this. Read the full legal breakdown at travelnoire.com. And download the Travel Noire app to connect with fellow expats who have already made the move and can share what life actually looks like on the ground. 📱 🔗

#TravelNoire #MoveToAfrica #BlackExpats #BlackTravel
